scope.summary:
Implement embed NodeViews for the four CKC media embed kinds (image, slideshow, album, video), porting the React embed asset resolution logic from app/src/lib/editor/embed_assets.ts and the hsLink NodeView rendering. Each embed kind is a BlockNode in the MT-011 DocModel with kind = EmbedNode, attrs = { embed_kind: String, ref_value: String }, and renders as an interactive egui widget inline in the document.

EXPECTED DIFF SHAPE:
- src/frontend/rich_editor/embeds/mod.rs
- src/frontend/rich_editor/embeds/asset_resolver.rs (async fetch of asset metadata + content URL from the real backend; mirrors embed_assets.ts logic)
- src/frontend/rich_editor/embeds/image_view.rs (single image renderer)
- src/frontend/rich_editor/embeds/slideshow_view.rs (ordered sequence of images with prev/next nav)
- src/frontend/rich_editor/embeds/album_view.rs (grid of images, click to enlarge)
- src/frontend/rich_editor/embeds/video_view.rs (video poster + play button; actual decode via OS media layer or a stub play button linking to the content URL)
- src/frontend/rich_editor/embeds/embed_block_renderer.rs (dispatch from EmbedNode attrs to the correct view)
- Extend src/frontend/rich_editor/renderer/block_renderer.rs to call embed_block_renderer for EmbedNode kind.

ASYNC ASSET RESOLUTION (asset_resolver.rs):
- Mirror the React embed_assets.ts validation pipeline EXACTLY (FAIL-CLOSED):
  - Reject empty ref_value -> EmbedError::EmptyRef
  - Reject absolute filesystem paths (drive letters, leading '/', UNC '\\') -> EmbedError::AbsolutePathRejected
  - Reject '..' or path separator chars in asset_id -> EmbedError::TraversalRejected
  - Reject http/https/file/javascript schemes -> EmbedError::SchemeRejected
  - For album/slideshow: ref_value is comma-separated asset_id list; validate each entry individually
- HTTP call: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/assets/{asset_id} -> EmbedAssetMetadata
- HTTP call: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/assets/{asset_id}/content -> raw bytes for image display
- HTTP call: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/assets/{asset_id}/thumbnail -> thumbnail bytes
- Use reqwest (already in workspace) or the existing handshake REST client; do NOT add a new HTTP crate.
- Resolution is async; use tokio::spawn in the egui event loop's async context or the shell's runtime. Cache resolved assets in a HashMap<String, EmbedResolutionState> keyed by asset_id so repeated renders do not re-fetch.

EMBED STATES (match React EmbedResolution):
- Resolving: show a spinner (egui::Spinner)
- Ok(asset, bytes): render the appropriate view
- Err(EmbedError): show a typed error chip (colored rounded rect with error kind text, never a blank) with author_id = 'embed-error-{asset_id}'

VIEW IMPLEMENTATIONS:
- image_view.rs: decode image bytes with the 'image' crate (PNG/JPEG/WebP/GIF); upload as egui::TextureHandle via ctx.load_texture(); render at aspect-correct width (max = available content width). Click opens full-size overlay.
- slideshow_view.rs: render one image at a time with prev/next arrow buttons; store current_index in view state. AccessKit: author_id = 'slideshow-{asset_id}', prev button = 'slideshow-prev-{asset_id}', next = 'slideshow-next-{asset_id}'.
- album_view.rs: render thumbnails in a wrapping grid (3 per row default). Click on a thumbnail opens a full-size modal (egui::Window). AccessKit: grid container = 'album-{ref_value}', each cell = 'album-cell-{asset_id}'.
- video_view.rs: render thumbnail with a play button overlay. On play click, open the content URL in the OS default player via open::that() (the 'open' crate) OR render a simple progress-bar placeholder with 'Video: {filename}' text. Mark this as a known limitation in code comments; full in-process video decode is deferred.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Add to frontend Cargo.toml: image = { version='0.25', features=['png','jpeg','webp','gif'] }, open = '5' (for video OS open).
2. Implement EmbedError enum and EmbedResolutionState in asset_resolver.rs. Write async fn resolve_embed(workspace_id, ref_value, embed_kind) -> EmbedResolution.
3. In embed_block_renderer.rs, match embed_kind to dispatch to the correct view fn. Pass the view state (HashMap per embed node, stored in RichEditorState).
4. In image_view.rs, cache the decoded TextureHandle in view state keyed by asset_id to avoid re-decoding every frame.
5. In block_renderer.rs (from MT-012), add a match arm for NodeKind::Embed that calls embed_block_renderer::render_embed(ui, node, embed_states, workspace_id).
6. Write cargo tests: asset_resolver validates and rejects absolute paths, traversal, empty refs (unit tests, no real HTTP). Integration test with a real backend: resolve a known asset_id and verify EmbedResolutionState::Ok is returned.
7. Assign AccessKit NodeIds and roles to all interactive embed controls as specified above.

DEPENDENCIES: MT-011 (EmbedNode kind in DocModel), MT-012 (block_renderer integration point). No dependency on MT-013 (formatting).
acceptance_criteria: An EmbedNode with embed_kind='images' and a valid asset_id renders a decoded image at correct aspect ratio in a kittest screenshot (requires a real backend with a seeded test asset).
An EmbedNode with an empty ref_value renders a typed error chip 'empty_ref' (not blank) in the kittest screenshot.
An EmbedNode with ref_value containing '..' is rejected with EmbedError::TraversalRejected before any HTTP call is made (unit test, no backend required).
An EmbedNode with ref_value starting with 'http://' is rejected with EmbedError::SchemeRejected (unit test).
Slideshow prev/next buttons cycle through 3 images in order; after the last image, next wraps to the first (cargo test with mock resolution states).
Album grid renders 3 thumbnails per row; clicking a thumbnail shows a full-size modal (kittest interaction).
Video embed renders a play button overlay; clicking it calls open::that() with the content URL (unit test with a mock open fn).
AccessKit: 'slideshow-prev-{asset_id}' and 'slideshow-next-{asset_id}' buttons are present in the kittest accessibility tree.
All resolved assets are cached: a second render of the same embed does NOT issue a second HTTP request (unit test with a counted mock fetcher).
cargo test -- embeds passes with 0 failures.
proof_targets: cargo test --package handshake_frontend -- embeds outputs 'test result: ok'.

Integration test (requires real Postgres backend): cargo test --test native_editors -- embeds::test_real_image_resolve -- --ignored (runs only when backend is available); output shows asset metadata fetched and texture decoded.
kittest screenshot saved to tests/native_editors/screenshots/mt014_image_embed.png showing a rendered image with correct aspect ratio.
kittest screenshot saved to tests/native_editors/screenshots/mt014_embed_error.png showing the typed error chip for an invalid ref.
implementation_notes: The 'image' crate decode is CPU-heavy for large images. Decode on a tokio::spawn_blocking thread, then send the decoded RGBA bytes to the egui context via a channel/callback. Never decode on the egui UI thread.
egui TextureHandle must be created on the egui thread (ctx.load_texture); only the RGBA bytes travel across the thread boundary.
Album/slideshow ref_value is a comma-separated list of asset_ids (matching the React parseAssetRefList function in embed_assets.ts). Split on comma, trim whitespace, validate each entry individually.

The 'open' crate is used for video OS-level playback; this is an explicit known limitation documented in video_view.rs. In-process video decode requires a future MT with a media decoder crate (e.g. symphonia + ffmpeg bindings) and is out of scope here.
Asset resolution cache (HashMap<String, EmbedResolutionState>) must be stored in RichEditorState (owned by the shell frame), not inside the renderer functions, so it persists across frames.
Fail-closed: any EmbedError must render a VISIBLE typed chip. Never render blank space, never panic. This matches the React embed_assets.ts EmbedErrorKind contract.
ports_from_react: app/src/lib/editor/embed_assets.ts (EmbedError kinds, path/scheme validation logic, asset metadata URL pattern, parseAssetRefList for album/slideshow)
app/src/components/HsLinkView.tsx (React NodeView that renders image/video/album/slideshow embeds inline in Tiptap; this MT is the native equivalent)
app/src/lib/editor/embed_assets.ts (EmbedResolverContext.workspaceId -> native: workspace_id passed through RichEditorState)
binds_backend_api: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/assets/{asset_id} -> EmbedAssetMetadata (asset metadata: mime, size, width, height, original_filename)

risks: Large image decode on the UI thread freezes the egui frame loop for hundreds of milliseconds.
Album grid with 50+ thumbnails issues 50+ concurrent HTTP requests on first render, overwhelming the backend.
A malicious ref_value containing a relative path escaping the asset namespace (e.g. '../../etc/passwd') bypasses the validation check if the check only looks for leading slash.
Video OS open() call succeeds but opens the wrong application if the content URL is a localhost URL that the OS does not associate with a media player.
TextureHandle created from a failed decode (empty bytes) causes egui to panic or show a corrupted texture rect.
minimum_controls: Image decode MUST be on tokio::spawn_blocking; a cargo test verifies the UI thread is not blocked by timing the render call with a 10 MB image.
Album/slideshow must limit concurrent resolution to 6 async tasks at a time using a tokio::Semaphore; a test with 12 asset_ids asserts at most 6 simultaneous in-flight requests.
Validation must reject any ref_value containing '..' as a substring ANYWHERE in the string (not just as a path component start); unit test: '..hidden/secret' is rejected.
Video view must show the content URL as text alongside the play button so the operator can inspect it; the OS open call is gated on explicit user click only.

Decode failure (image::load_from_memory returns Err) must transition to EmbedResolutionState::Err(EmbedError::MediaLoadFailed) and render the error chip; unit test verifies with intentionally corrupt bytes.
